note: Dedicated dashboard health agent started per Founder spec. Initial cycle scope: dashboard.html + amendments.html + proposals.html + escalations.html + activity.html + token-usage.html + discussion-bubbles.html + design-system.html + index.html. Excludes main-flows.html (3rd parallel agent).

summary: Dashboard-not-updating-per-commit root cause: Git core.hooksPath = .husky/_ (Husky redirect). AMD-019 hook at .git/hooks/post-commit was bypassed silently — Husky trampoline looks for .husky/post-commit which did not exist. Local dashboard mtime lagged latest commit by 5-6 minutes (94e4340 + 66a6e19 produced no regen output). The fresh-per-commit pipeline has been dead since AMD-019 install.

Installed .husky/post-commit (chmod +x). Runs regen subset (excludes regen-main-flows.py per 3rd-agent scope split). Includes AMD-020 Class A auto-clean restricted to dashboard regen output (docs/reports/dashboard|amendments|proposals|escalations|activity|token-usage|discussion-bubbles|design-system|index.html + .claude/state/telemetry/aggregates). Excludes docs/reports/main-flows.html + .claude/state/main-flows-v2/ + .claude/state/telemetry/events (events handled by watcher allowlist already).

End-to-end test: commit e6f2433 ([dashboard-health] install) fired new hook; hook produced auto-commit 0b9798b (cron(routine): post-commit dashboard regen) with 5 HTMLs + 3 aggregate JSONs. team-work summary event emitted at 22:36:51 with note='post-commit-hook'. Dashboard data block as_of=22:36:51.919Z. dashboard.html mtime=18:36:54 EDT, matches commit. Pipeline confirmed live.

AMD-019 amendment text specifies hook location as .git/hooks/post-commit — but actual working location is .husky/post-commit (Git core.hooksPath redirects). Amendment text needs update for accuracy. Out of my scope per agent spec (substrate amendments = main agent territory). Surfacing here for next pass.

Live dashboard URL is file:///C:/Users/Zach/smoky-mountain-open/docs/reports/dashboard.html (per Founder clarification). GH Pages https://alrightlad.github.io/smoky-mountain-open/reports/dashboard.html returns 404 — Vite build only includes index.html via deploy.yml. Health checks adapt: file content + mtime checks instead of Playwright vs live URL.

Founder directive: 'only I should have access to dashboard and it should all be local not shared for anyone else to see'. Committed 8eb0a15: added docs/reports/*.html + _assets/ to .gitignore, git-rm-cached all 16 paths. Files preserved on disk; tracking stopped. Post-commit hook's CLASS_A allowlist still references the now-gitignored docs/reports paths — harmless (git diff against gitignored returns clean), allowlist entries are dead but reversible.

docs/reports/*.html still present in git history on origin/main (many commits up to and including 0b9798b which is local-only). Pushed history is public. Founder decision needed: scrub via filter-branch / BFG, or accept that historical dashboards are exposed.

Aggregates (.claude/state/telemetry/aggregates/*.json), events (.claude/state/telemetry/events/*.ndjson), main-flows-v2/, app-audit-* dirs, and other substrate state remain tracked. They contain source data that feeds dashboards. If Founder wants dashboard PRIVATE, source data is the same level of sensitivity. Surfacing for next-cycle decision.

Verified: post-commit hook fired after 8eb0a15 (gitignore commit) → auto-commit 044bd63 included only aggregates (3 JSONs), no docs/reports paths (gitignored). Local dashboard files preserved on disk (docs/reports/dashboard.html exists, dashboard.css exists). Pipeline working as designed under new privacy posture.

checks.hook_fires_on_commit: PASS — efdbea9 (main agent's commit) triggered 7f44f90 auto-commit
checks.watcher_skip_reason: skip-dirty (main agent's src/pages/courses.js + iter-16 PNGs) — expected, not a failure

summary: All checks pass. Pipeline live after AMD-019 hook relocation + gitignore privacy lock.

note: Initial cycle complete. Infrastructure persists across turns (hook installed, gitignore committed, log directory established). Next cycle on Founder re-invocation. Per AMD-017 continuation: this is a legitimate turn-end (initial setup + verification complete; subsequent cycles are read-only verification unless hook regresses).

summary: Founder spun up Terminal 4 (Test/QA) + Terminal 5 (Security/Compliance). New directive: render Test Health + Security Health banners on dashboard.html reading from .claude/state/aggregates/{test,security}-health.json. Layout: top of dashboard alongside existing infra status. Click expands details.

Added two pb-kpi-card banners (Test health + Security health) in dashboard.html System Health subsection — sit alongside round-trip/working-tree/halts cards. Status dot colored green/yellow/red/unknown per agent's status field. Click expands inline detail panel below the grid (counts chips + details list + links). regen-dashboard.py extended with read_health_banner() — tolerant of multiple schema conventions (as_of OR timestamp; summary OR status_reason; counts dict OR active_findings OR top-level checks_* numerics; details array OR known_failures/regressions/vulnerable_deps/credential_leaks).

Both agents already writing files. Test Health: 'yellow · 5/8 checks passing · 2 skipped · 1 known failure (user-context-gate)' as of 22:48:18Z. Security Health: 'yellow · 2 HIGH-severity dependency vulnerabilities in functions/' as of 22:46:49Z. Both populate correctly in dashboard data block.

Structural HTML changes to dashboard.html (banner cards, CSS, JS) are LOCAL-ONLY since the file is gitignored per privacy directive. If Founder migrates to another machine: regen-dashboard.py + READMEs persist (tracked); dashboard.html base HTML would come from origin/main (pre-banners). Recovery: re-apply banner structure manually OR refactor regen-dashboard.py to use a template (deferred — not blocking).
